The Company reported that a flow meter has not been installed due to the site being shut-in for large portions of time, although production was reported from Jul-23 through Aug- 24, violating Regulation 7, Part B § II.B.2.g.

Source failed to route all hydrocarbon emissions to the ECDs and operate without venting emissions from tank access points on nineteen (19) occasions, as identified in the table below, violating Regulation 7, Part B § II.C.2.a. The Design Analysis (DA) provided was predicated on control by two (2) 48” IES combustors and one (1) 144” QTI combustor. However, the QTI combustor has been shut in since July 2023. Therefore, the DA failed to be representative of the Facilities’ design and operation.

Source failed to design, operate, and maintain the Facility to minimize emissions to atmosphere to the maximum extent practicable. Operate and maintain: in the source response, Extraction stated that no operation and maintenance program was in place to routinely observe facility operating parameters. The Source also stated that no specific operating parameters that affect the capacity of the STVCS were identified and that there was no monitoring plan implemented to ensure that the facility operated in alignment with the DA. Design: the DA and Predictive Analysis provided were based on the following control equipment: two (2) IES ECDs and one (1) QTI ECD. The DA additionally was evaluated based on two (2) active wells (the number active when the DA was completed) and then on fourteen (14) active wells (representative of future buildout). These specifications are not accurate of the Facility at the time of the nineteen (19) venting events in this report’s nine (9) month compliance period (see table above). The Facility operated with nine (9) active wells during the compliance period and only the two (2) IES ECDs. The Source failed to update the DA and Predictive Analysis when the Facility makeup changed, such as shutting in the QTI combustor in July 2023 and bringing on new wells. The Predictive Analysis was last completed on 10/28/2022 and the DA was last revised 5/21/2020, these outdated analyses fail to account for changes to the Facility. The DA states that “the model should be re-evaluated if: the quantity and/or configuration of combustors was different than what is assumed for this analysis. The quantity of wells planned for full buildout is different than what is assumed for this analysis.” Finally, the DA failed to clearly list the critical operating parameters that should be monitored to ensure over pressurization does not occur.
